vocab.meaning: học hỏi/làm thử một cách không chuyên (tập tành)
vocab.example: Although I dabble in watercolor painting as a hobby, I don't think I have the discipline to be a full-time artist.

vocab.meaning: gợi hình, gợi cảm, gợi lên nhiều liên tưởng
vocab.example: I’ve always admired artists who can create evocative landscapes that transport the viewer to another world.
I lack the moody artistic temperament usually associated with great painters; I’m far too pragmatic.
Being an artist is a powerful form of visual storytelling that can transcend language barriers.
The ability to translate an abstract concept into a physical sculpture is a talent I find truly enviable.

vocab.meaning: thu hút được sự công nhận/nổi tiếng
vocab.example: It takes years of grueling work before an artist can finally garner recognition in the elite art circles.

vocab.meaning: vờn sáng và tối (kỹ thuật trong hội họa/nhiếp ảnh)
vocab.example: I love how some artists play with light and shadow to create a sense of drama and mystery in their work.

vocab.meaning: chi phí đắt đỏ (gây cản trở)
vocab.example: The prohibitive costs of high-quality oil paints and studio space make it difficult for many to pursue art professionally.

vocab.meaning: vẻ đẹp thẩm mỹ, sự lôi cuốn về mặt nhìn
vocab.example: While some focus on the message, I’m more interested in the aesthetic appeal of a piece—how it brightens a room.

vocab.meaning: nuôi dưỡng/thúc đẩy sự sáng tạo
vocab.example: I wouldn't want it as a job, but I do think it's important to engage in art to foster creativity in daily life.
